title: potentilla minima var. brauneana
intro: Potentilla minima var. brauneana, also known as dwarf potentilla, is a low-growing, shrubby perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
